raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "ACA 1 proposes to reduce the local vote threshold for bond approval and special tax measures from a two-thirds vote to a 55 percent supermajority.",
    "Current constitutional supermajority vote thresholds restrict local officials, especially in small cities and rural areas, from making investments in local infrastructure priorities.",
    "Many voters in California have supported local propositions for secure funding for public safety, but these propositions have often fallen short despite winning a majority of the vote.",
    "ACA 1 aims to create a more democratic process by allowing voters to approve measures with a 55 percent threshold instead of the current two-thirds requirement.",
    "Lowering the bond approval threshold to 55 percent will enable local governments to invest in vital projects and create thousands of good-paying jobs.",
    "The California Constitution currently limits communities' ability to tax themselves for local projects to meet approved goals, allowing one-third of local voters to overrule fiscal decisions."
  ]
}
```
